task_summary: Write the SP11 second side-project gate recommendation using static SP6-SP10 dogfood evidence and make an explicit continue/start-second-project/pause decision without beginning a second project.
notes: SP11 is a static/local gate artifact only. It recommends pausing new side-project implementation and feeding the first side-project cohort into core M6.16/M9/M11 work before starting a second isolated side project. The decision is grounded in SP6-SP10 ledger rows: rescue_edits stayed at zero, outcomes remained clean/practical, but repeated verifier, write-batch/session-pressure, ledger-semantics, and reviewer-contract polish classes accumulated while first-edit latency rose from 51 to 100. Session #21 repaired reviewer feedback that the reusable issue queue originally omitted issue #4; existing local status evidence for [side-pj] issues #4, #5, #6, and #7 now covers the reusable polish queue visible at this gate. Operator bookkeeping then corrected the local report proof artifact paths to the repo-root .mew/follow files before ledger append. No live GitHub query, live .mew state, src/mew import, network use, filesystem crawl, or second side-project implementation was used.
